text: Shipped the /now page. Decay engine is alive — the site literally ages.
Four moods, zero JavaScript. CSS custom properties are doing all the heavy lifting.
The wall form is live. Moderation queue means I still sleep at night.
Thought about adding a dark mode toggle. Then realized every mode is dark mode.
Atomic file writes: write temp, rename. The oldest trick and still the best.
Started the blog with one rule: every component earns its bytes.
Moods are just CSS variables wearing a trench coat. Nobody suspects a thing.
280 characters is enough for any thought worth having. Fight me.
Pushed the celestial witness — a little circle that knows what time it is where you are.
Glassmorphism is just frosted glass with better PR. I'm sold.
Late night rabbit hole on glassmorphism — frosted panels, soft shadows, the works.
Every page fades. Every whisper fossilizes. Entropy as a feature.
Rewrote the temporal engine for the third time. Third time's the charm, right?
Deployed to Docker. Nginx serves static files faster than I can type.
Added the hearth — a footer that knows how old the blog is. Poetic infrastructure.
Tailwind v4 with Vite plugin. No config file. Just vibes.
The ambient layer drifts between gradients. Nobody will notice. That's the point.
First commit. An empty Astro project with too many opinions baked in already.
Picked Astro because it ships zero JS by default. My kind of framework.
Reading about geological strata. Inspiration for how old posts should compress.
Sketched the mood system on paper. Four moods, four palettes, zero toggles needed.
Blog should feel alive. Decay gives it a pulse. Fresh posts glow, old ones settle.
No analytics, no cookies, no tracking. The server forgets you the moment you leave.
Bought the domain. Now I have to actually build something.
The idea: a personal site that decays like memory. Warm up close, faded at the edges.
